skill.name: web-search-scraper-api-skill
skill.description: Extract readable Markdown content from a web page by calling the BrowserAct Web Search Scraper API with a target URL.
skill.summary: Extracts web page content as Markdown through the BrowserAct scraper API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static findings for network access, environment access, and command execution are mostly expected for this API-backed scraper skill. No evidence of prompt injection, obfuscation, unauthorized credential exfiltration, or weak cryptography was found, but the skill sends a bearer token and target URL to an external BrowserAct endpoint.

medium_findings.title: External API Receives User URL and Bearer Token
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY, creates an Authorization bearer header, and sends the user supplied target URL to the BrowserAct workflow API. This is intended behavior, but users must trust the external service with the URL and API key.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network request and bearer header are explicit in the script. The behavior appears legitimate for the skill, so the risk is trust and data exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Skill Instructs Agent to Request an API Key
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the agent to ask the user for BROWSERACT_API_KEY when it is missing, and the script prints guidance to provide or set the key. This is a credential handling workflow that should be disclosed clearly to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files directly instruct API key handling. No evidence shows misuse, but credential collection by an agent is a meaningful marketplace risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Example Requires Local Script Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections in SKILL.md are mainly Markdown formatting and one intended Python invocation. The command is static and does not concatenate untrusted shell input, but running bundled scripts is still a minor operational risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command example is visible and uses a fixed Python script path with a URL argument. I did not find shell metacharacter concatenation or dynamic shell construction.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections map to Markdown-related text and documentation lines. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced file is documentation for Markdown extraction. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic implementation appears at those locations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections refer to task identifier handling and documentation text about checking output. No host inventory, process listing, or local system discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines do not perform local discovery. They handle a remote task ID and describe error handling text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-Bearing HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The script builds an Authorization bearer header from the API key and uses it on POST and GET requests to a hardcoded external API. This is expected for the integration but should remain transparent to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bearer header and HTTP calls are explicit. The pattern is sensitive but aligned with the documented BrowserAct API workflow.

content.value_statement: Web pages are hard to reuse when the useful text is mixed with layout and scripts. This skill calls BrowserAct to extract readable Markdown from a supplied URL.
actual_capabilities: Runs a Python script that submits a URL to the BrowserAct workflow API.
Reads the BrowserAct API key from the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.
Polls the BrowserAct task status until completion or timeout.
Prints extracted Markdown content when the API returns a result string.
Reports missing API keys, task failures, polling errors, and timeouts.
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key before it can run.
Sends the target URL and API key to the BrowserAct service.
Can take several minutes because it polls a remote browser workflow.
Does not validate whether scraping a target page is permitted.

use_cases.title: Collect Article Text
use_cases.description: Extract the main Markdown content from a news article or blog post for review.

use_cases.title: Archive Documentation Pages
use_cases.description: Convert a tutorial or documentation page into Markdown for offline reference.

use_cases.title: Prepare Source Material
use_cases.description: Turn a web page into cleaner text before summarization or analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Extract One Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-search-scraper-api-skill to extract Markdown from this URL: [URL].
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single web page.

prompt_templates.title: Extract and Summarize
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract Markdown from [URL], then give me a short summary and the main headings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that combines extraction with review.

prompt_templates.title: Research a Documentation Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract Markdown from [URL]. Identify setup steps, prerequisites, and warnings from the page.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Source Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract Markdown from [URL], preserve source structure, and list claims that need verification.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for careful source analysis.

output_examples.input: Extract Markdown from a product documentation page.
output_examples.output: A Markdown version of the page with headings, paragraphs, links, and the main content preserved.

output_examples.input: Extract and summarize a news article.
output_examples.output: Clean article text followed by a concise summary and key points from the extracted content.

output_examples.input: Turn a tutorial URL into notes.
output_examples.output: Structured Markdown notes that keep the tutorial steps and important warnings readable.

best_practices: Use this skill only for pages you are allowed to access and process.
Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY in the environment instead of pasting it into prompts.
Review extracted content before using it as a factual source.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to bypass access controls, paywalls, or site restrictions.
Do not submit private, sensitive, or regulated URLs without approval.
Do not treat extracted Markdown as verified truth without checking the source.

faq.question: What does this skill extract?
faq.answer: It extracts readable Markdown content from a supplied web page URL through the BrowserAct API.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires BROWSERACT_API_KEY to authenticate requests to BrowserAct.

faq.question: Where does the URL go?
faq.answer: The script sends the target URL to the BrowserAct workflow API for remote processing.

faq.question: Can it scrape any website?
faq.answer: It can accept HTTP and HTTPS URLs, but users must respect site terms and access rules.

faq.question: What happens if the task takes too long?
faq.answer: The script polls for up to 300 seconds, then reports a timeout if no result is ready.

faq.question: Is the extracted content always complete?
faq.answer: No. Completeness depends on the page structure, BrowserAct processing, and access permissions.
